pfc:
## 1. Lamination
Pre-bond films and backers to face materials before cutting; web temps/dwell set by factory.
1. Laminate SWOOSH BACKER / LAMINATION hot-melt film to the back of the San Fang Smiley-Cygni 1.0mm TPU swoosh stock at [factory: temp/time/pressure].
2. Laminate REINFORCEMENT BACKER (HIRUN D ZONES) hot-melt film to Baiksan Hirun D .09mm reinforcement sheet (lateral + medial).
3. [+] Laminate RIP-STOP TPU SHROUD (SSF TPU TEX-1, foil + coating) to a low-temp carrier film for dimensional stability before cutting.
4. Roll-cool and rest laminated panels flat before die cutting to prevent curl.

## 2. Cutting — Die Schedule
Die-cut all sheet/film components; cutting pressure and die clearance by factory.
1. Cut SYNTHETIC REINFORCEMENT (LATERAL) and (MEDIAL) from backed Baiksan Hirun D using the Hirun D cutting die.
2. Cut SWOOSH (LATERAL) and (MEDIAL) blanks from backed Smiley-Cygni TPU.
3. Cut HOLOGRAM FILM DETAIL, HOLOGRAM TAPE, and HOLOGRAM TAPE 6MM (LATERAL/MEDIAL) from Duragon Stretch .23mm using the hologram tape dies.
4. Cut RIP-STOP TPU SHROUD throat panel.
5. [+] Cut CENTER-SEAM REINFORCEMENT TAPE to length for the bootie center seam.
6. Cut STROBEL BOARD and COLLAR LINING blanks at [factory: die clearance].

## 3. Skiving & Perforation
Edge-prep bonded parts for clean overlays; skive depths by factory.
1. Skive perimeter of SWOOSH (lateral/medial) blanks for flush bonding to Flyknit.
2. Skive edges of SYNTHETIC REINFORCEMENT (lateral/medial) at overlap zones.
3. [+] Skive RIP-STOP TPU SHROUD bonding margins where it overlaps the Flyknit throat.

## 4. Logo, Screen Print & HF Welding
Apply graphics and weld TPU parts to Flyknit; all heat steps factory-tuned.
1. Screenprint ALPHA SCREENPRINT (LATERAL) and (MEDIAL) onto Flyknit quarters; cure at [factory: temp/time].
2. Screenprint HEEL PULL TAB graphic on woven tab stock.
3. HF-weld SWOOSH (LATERAL) and (MEDIAL) to the Flyknit upper using the HF-weld swoosh die at [factory: temp/time/pressure].
4. HF-weld CORD-RELEASE LOGO (Littlefoot triangle) at the cable release position.
5. Heat-apply HOLOGRAM TAPE, 6MM tapes, and HOLOGRAM FILM DETAIL to designated lateral/medial placements.

## 5. Computer Stitching
Embroider/stitch programmed reinforcements onto flat upper; stitch density by factory.
1. Stitch SYNTHETIC REINFORCEMENT (lateral/medial) to Flyknit at high-wear zones.
2. Stitch LAMINATED CABLE PADS channels per Littlefoot (Alpha 4) cable routing.
3. [+] Tack LITTLEFOOT LACE CABLE and STRETCH CORD (SECONDARY) guide loops at programmed coordinates.

## 6. Upper Assembly & Strobel Close
Build the Flyknit bootie, close center seam, attach collar and structure.
1. Join the center-seam Flyknit upper and reinforce with CENTER-SEAM REINFORCEMENT TAPE via hot-melt.
2. Assemble BOOTIE COLLAR / INNER SLEEVE (Flyknit) with COLLAR FOAM and COLLAR LINING.
3. Attach HEEL PULL TAB at heel center.
4. Insert TOE STIFFENER and HEEL COUNTER; activate at [factory: temp/time].
5. Attach LASTING / GUSSET TAPE around the lasting margin.
6. Strobel-stitch STROBEL BOARD to the bootie bottom to close the sock for the drop-in midsole seat.
7. Route LITTLEFOOT LACE CABLE through the Alpha 4 mechanism and verify cable release function.

## 7. Plate Injection (Outside Vendor)
Injected TPU cleated plate produced at Evertech; mold parameters by vendor.
1. Inject OUTSOLE PLATE (CLEATED) in AZZ439 TPU at Evertech using the new cleated plate mold at [factory: melt temp/cycle].
2. De-flash, trim sprue, and inspect cleat geometry against last X3208H-Core bottom.
3. [+] Confirm AZZ439 material certification and plate hardness with Evertech before lot release.

## 8. EVA / Phylon Midsole
New drop-in midsole molded at FT; durometer and density by factory.
1. Mold DROP-IN MIDSOLE foam at [factory: durometer/density].
2. Laminate DROP-IN MIDSOLE TOP CLOTH to the foam top surface.
3. Trim drop-in perimeter to seat into the lasted bootie cavity.

## 9. Stockfitting (Midsole → Outsole)
Bond detail pods to the plate to form the bottom unit; cement system factory-set.
1. Buff/prime OUTSOLE PLATE (CLEATED) bonding surfaces.
2. Cement and press MOLDED RUBBER DETAIL PODS to the plate at [factory: cement/press time].
3. Inspect bottom unit for pod adhesion and flash.

## 10. Sockliner
Build the drop-in sockliner; foam spec by factory.
1. Mold SOCKLINER / INSOLE in EVA.
2. Laminate SOCKLINER TOP CLOTH to the sockliner.
3. Die-cut and emboss size/logo as required.

## 11. Assembly — Lasting & Bottom Attach
Last the bootie and attach the bottom unit; activation and press by factory.
1. Slip the closed Flyknit bootie onto last X3208H-Core.
2. Heat-set the upper and shape toe/heel at [factory: temp/time].
3. Seat the DROP-IN MIDSOLE into the lasted bootie.
4. Apply PLATE-TO-BOOTIE CEMENT and primer to mating surfaces of the bottom unit and lasted bootie.
5. Position bottom unit, press, and chill-set.

## 12. Cleated Toe Spring & QC
Verify cleat orientation, toe spring, and bond integrity.
1. Verify toe spring and cleat plane against last spec.
2. Pull-test plate-to-bootie bond and swoosh weld adhesion.
3. Function-check LITTLEFOOT LACE CABLE tension and release; insert SOCKLINER.
4. Final clean, lace, and visual QC against lead colorway (Black/White/Iron Grey).

## 13. Packing
Stuff, wrap, label, and carton per standard.
1. Insert TOE STUFFING and wrap with TISSUE PAPER.
2. Apply C/O LABEL with size/country data.
3. Box pair in INNER BOX.
4. Pack into OUTER CARTON per [factory: pack ratio/qty].
